page: Microsoft Azure DS13_v2 is a DSv2-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this server features 35 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AES cryptography, and FMA. The system is provisioned with 56.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 115 GB of local storage. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 720.03 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 76.69 GB/sec, both representing average tier performance.

From a resource density perspective, the DS13_v2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity without demanding top-tier single-thread CPU speed. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core processing power, which limits its suitability for latency-sensitive or single-threaded applications. However, with average multi-core performance, solid database operations, and steady Redis SET throughput of 11.85 million operations per second, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DS13_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 56.0 GB of memory and 115 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.0 GB per core. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core CPU execution, but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, with cached read-only memory bandwidth reaching 720.03 GB/sec. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and AES, which assist in cryptographic and vector calculations. Multi-threaded performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 5978.0 and a PassMark database operations score of 4601.14. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high single-threaded processing speeds, making it best suited for database hosting, Redis deployments, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS13_v2 is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 Intel Xeon cores, 56 GB RAM, and 115 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS13_v2 features 8 Intel Xeon cores, 56 GB RAM, and 115 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual server offering high memory density and balanced multi-core performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL cores running at 2.6 GHz
56.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
115 GB of local storage with no bundled GPU
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Best suited for database hosting, Redis, and static web serving
